extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by versus against each category: I counted only the Against Company, Against Directors, Against Promoter and Against Subsidiary rows. Criminal proceedings against total 33 (32 against the Company plus 1 against Directors), statutory/regulatory proceedings against total 2 (1 against the Company plus 1 against Directors), and tax proceedings against the Company and Subsidiary total 16 (16 against the Company and nil against the Subsidiary); all By rows were excluded. The quantified aggregate amount against the Company is explicitly stated as ₹2,697.5 million; Directors and Subsidiary show no quantified amount, so this converts to ₹269.75 crore at 1 crore = ₹10 million. The contingent-liabilities figure is the stated consolidated-style Total of ₹143.6 million as of December 31, 2023, converted to ₹14.36 crore, rather than a re-sum. No company-wide top-five or top-ten customer revenue concentration percentage is disclosed; the only quantified concentrations relate to other measures such as state AUM and segment/customer information, not customers’ share of revenue from operations or total income. The source unit for the litigation and contingent-liability tables is ₹ million.
